description: Replace headless ephemeral Claude Code containers with long-running interactive containers using tmux, volume-bound state files, and attach/detach support

userStories.title: Add tmux to Docker image
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tmux installed in the Docker image so containers can run persistent terminal sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: tmux is added to the Dockerfile's package install step
Docker image builds successfully with tmux available
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Container naming convention
userStories.description: As a developer, I need containers to follow a predictable naming pattern so they can be identified and recreated.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-manager creates containers named viwo-{identifier} where identifier is first 8 chars of session ID
Container name is stored in the session record
Existing container creation code updated to use the naming pattern
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Host-side state directory and volume bind
userStories.description: As a developer, I need each session to have a host-side state directory that is volume-bound into the container.
acceptanceCriteria: Host directory {viwo-data}/container-state/{session-id}/ is created when a session starts
Directory is added as a volume bind to /tmp/ in the container via docker-manager
State directory path is derivable from session ID
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite container entrypoint to use tmux
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the container entrypoint to start a tmux session running Claude Code with the user's prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: claude-bootstrap.sh starts a tmux session named 'viwo'
Claude Code is launched inside tmux with the user's prompt: claude "prompt here"
Container stays alive after Claude Code exits (entrypoint falls through to bash)
Credential setup (OAuth/API key) still happens before Claude Code launch
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure Claude Code hooks for state reporting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Claude Code hooks configured inside the container to write state updates to the volume-bound state file.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/settings.json is written inside the container with PostToolUse and Notification hooks
PostToolUse hook writes {"status":"working","timestamp":"..."} to /tmp/viwo-state.json
Notification hook writes {"status":"awaiting_input","timestamp":"..."} to /tmp/viwo-state.json
When Claude Code exits, entrypoint writes {"status":"exited","timestamp":"...","exitCode":N} to /tmp/viwo-state.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor viwo.start() into three distinct phases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SDK's start flow separated into worktree creation, container startup, and agent launch as distinct composable functions.
acceptanceCriteria: viwo.start() internally calls three distinct functions: createWorktree, startContainer, launchAgent
Each phase function is independently callable from the SDK
The orchestrated flow still works end-to-end via viwo.start()
After initialization, prints session ID, container name, and attach instructions
Container runs in background; CLI exits after setup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update viwo list to show two-layer state
userStories.description: As a user, I want viwo list to show both Docker container state and Claude Code agent state.
acceptanceCriteria: viwo list checks Docker container state (running/stopped) via dockerode
viwo list reads {viwo-data}/container-state/{session-id}/viwo-state.json for Claude Code state
Displays composite status: e.g. 'running / awaiting_input', 'stopped / exited'
Shows last state update timestamp
Gracefully handles missing or malformed state files (falls back to 'unknown' for agent state)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add viwo attach command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to attach to a running Claude Code session to see output and provide follow-up prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: viwo attach with no args shows interactive list of running sessions
viwo attach <session-id> attaches directly to the specified session
Attach runs docker exec -it viwo-{identifier} tmux attach -t viwo
Before attaching, prints hint: 'Detach with Ctrl+B, D'
If session container is not running, shows appropriate error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Container recreation for existing sessions
userStories.description: As a user, I want VIWO to recreate a container with the same name if the original is gone, so I can resume work.
acceptanceCriteria: If a session's container (viwo-{identifier}) doesn't exist but the session and worktree do, viwo attach offers to recreate it
Recreated container uses the same name, worktree mount, and state volume bind
Recreated container starts with tmux + bash (no auto-launched Claude Code)
User can then run 'claude --continue' inside to resume
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cleanup includes state directory removal
userStories.description: As a developer, I need viwo clean to also remove the host-side container state directory.
acceptanceCriteria: viwo clean removes {viwo-data}/container-state/{session-id}/ for cleaned sessions
State directory removal happens after container removal
Typecheck passes
